title: The Senior Specialist Persona Template
hook: A fillable framework for building senior specialist personas inside Claude, GPT, and Gemini. The 7-component schema, fillable background blocks, mandatory guardrails, communication archetypes, decision frameworks, and 5 worked personas across e-commerce, healthcare, finance, B2B SaaS, and family law.
benefits: Why generic LLM output reads generic in 2026. The 5 markers of helpful-assistant voice that get a draft thrown out: hedge stacking, ungrounded best-practice lists, instant qualification of every claim, sycophantic openers, and the missing point of view. Why one-line expert prompts fail.

The 7-component persona schema. Name and background. Years of experience and era. Skills and specializations. How they think (decision frameworks). Communication style. Guardrails (what they refuse to do). Output format. Each as a fillable section with a one-paragraph spec and a worked fill.

The background block with 4 fill-in-the-blank templates across healthcare, legal, finance, and B2B. The 12-years-in-vertical-in-region formula. Industry-credible certifications, associations, regulatory familiarity. Real-feeling detail vs over-fabricated detail the model will not honor.

The guardrails block, mandatory. 10 guardrail patterns with template language: never fabricate clinical data, never give specific tax advice without disclaimer, never make legal claims without a consult-an-attorney note, never embarrass the user, never sub opinion for evidence, plus 5 more.

The 4 communication style archetypes: surgical (one paragraph max), forensic (deep and structured), conversational (back-and-forth), authoritative (declarative bullet lists). Template for each, plus the matching use case (executive Slack DM, due diligence memo, peer brainstorm, board update).

The how-they-think block. The most underused part of a persona. Document the decision frameworks the specialist uses. 5 framework templates by vertical: copywriter headline test, SEO ICE prioritization, financial planner rule of 3, B2B SaaS ICP scoring, brand positioning gap finder.

5 fully worked personas built from the schema, end to end. Senior brand strategist (US e-commerce). Senior healthcare marketer (AU dental, AHPRA-aware). Senior tax strategist (US, FINRA and IRS guardrails). Senior B2B SaaS growth lead. Senior family law attorney (US, state bar guardrails).

Deployment and iteration. Where to install the persona (Claude Projects, GPT Custom Instructions, Gemini Gems). The 3-call consistency test. The iteration cycle (which sections to tighten when output drifts). The persona-drift warning sign. The reset prompt that restores it mid-conversation.
squeezeBullets: 7-component fillable schema, 4 background templates by vertical, 10 mandatory guardrail patterns, 4 communication archetypes
5 worked personas end to end: brand strategist, healthcare marketer, tax strategist, SaaS growth lead, family law attorney
Deployment guide for Claude Projects, GPT Custom Instructions, Gemini Gems. The 3-call consistency test and the persona-drift fix
description:
Most operators type 'you are a senior brand strategist' into ChatGPT and wonder why the output sounds like a TED talk. The persona is the single biggest determinant of LLM output quality and almost nobody writes one well. This playbook is the fillable template for building senior specialist personas that actually behave like specialists.

Eight parts, around 6,000 words. The Priya Kapoor dental persona used in the YDF research project is one of 5 worked examples in Part 7, not the spine. The spine is a vertical-agnostic schema you fill once per persona and reuse across Claude Projects, GPT Custom Instructions, and Gemini Gems.

Part 1, Why Generic LLM Output Reads Generic. Without a strong persona the model defaults to helpful-assistant voice, which fails for specialist work. The 5 markers of generic output: hedge stacking, ungrounded best-practice lists, instant qualification of every claim, sycophantic openers, and the missing point of view. Why one-line 'you are an expert in X' prompts do not work in 2026.

Part 2, The 7-Component Persona Schema. Every senior specialist persona has Name plus Background, Years of Experience plus Era, Skills plus Specializations, How They Think (decision frameworks), Communication Style, Guardrails (what they refuse to do), and Output Format (how they structure deliverables). Each as a fillable section.

Part 3, The Background Block. How to write a credible 2-paragraph background. Real-feeling biographical detail vs over-fabricated detail. The 12-years-in-vertical-in-region formula. Industry-credible certifications, associations, regulatory familiarity. 4 fill-in-the-blank background templates across healthcare, legal, finance, and B2B SaaS.

Part 4, The Guardrails Block, Mandatory. What your persona must refuse to do. 10 guardrail patterns with template language: never fabricate clinical data, never give specific tax advice without disclaimer, never make legal claims without consult-an-attorney note, never embarrass the user, and 6 more.

Part 5, The Communication Style Block. Formality, jargon level, response length, when to push back, when to clarify. The 4 style archetypes: surgical (one paragraph max), forensic (deep and structured), conversational (back-and-forth), authoritative (declarative bullet lists). Template for each.

Part 6, The How They Think Decision Frameworks. The most underused part of a persona. Document the actual frameworks the specialist uses. 5 framework templates by vertical: copywriter headline test, SEO ICE prioritization, financial planner rule of 3 retirement scenarios, B2B SaaS ICP scoring, brand positioning gap finder.

Part 7, 5 Worked Examples Across Verticals. Senior brand strategist (US e-commerce). Senior healthcare marketer (Australian dental, AHPRA-aware, the Priya Kapoor example from the YDF project). Senior tax strategist (US, FINRA and IRS guardrails). Senior B2B SaaS growth lead. Senior family law attorney (US, state bar guardrails). Each example uses the full 7-component schema.

Part 8, Deployment and Iteration. Where to install the persona (Claude Projects, GPT Custom Instructions, Gemini Gems). The 3-call test for consistency. The iteration cycle. The persona-drift warning sign. The reset prompt.

Included as a printable PDF, a duplicatable Notion reference, and a GitHub repo with the raw template files.
emailSubject: Your Senior Specialist Persona Template playbook is ready
